title: V838 Mon: light echo evolution and distance estimate
use_role: primary light echo radius/center evolution source for 2002-2004
main_points: Measured light echo expansion from 2002 May to 2004 December; compared thin sheet and thin shell models; inferred dust likely interstellar thin sheet; distance around 9 kpc and star-dust distance about 5 pc.

title: NASA Light Echoes from V838 Mon
use_role: public explanatory reference for the light echo phenomenon
main_points: Describes 2002 flash and the apparent outward light echo as illumination of ambient interstellar dust rather than matter physically expanding at that apparent speed.

title: NASA/Hubble epoch image references for V838 Mon
use_role: epoch image support for 2002-2004 visual light echo evolution
main_points: Supports the use of 2002-2004 epoch light echo imagery as a direct visual reference series for expansion and morphology change.

title: The 2002 outburst of V838 Mon: as cool as it gets
main_points: Summarizes the 2002 event, notes multiple peaks, very cool supergiant-like evolution, and cites shell mass estimates on the order of about 1e-7 to 1e-5 solar masses from spectroscopic interpretation.
